title: hieracium cordifolium subsp. sericifolium
intro: Hieracium cordifolium subsp. sericifolium, also known as the heart-leaved hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, primarily used to treat skin conditions and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing swelling and promoting tissue repair, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies for skin irritations, minor cuts, and scrapes. Herbal preparations of Hieracium cordifolium subsp. sericifolium often involve infusions or tinctures made from the leaves and flowers, which can be applied topically or used in teas to support overall well-being.
